cards.text: AB + BC + AC is perimeter of  of triangle ABC where AB, BC and AC are calculated using distance formula.
If points A, B and C are vertices of isosceles triangle then AB = AC where AB and AC are calculated using distance formula.
If points A, B, C and D are vertices of rectangle then AB = CD & BC = AD where AB, BC, CD and AD are calculated using distance formula.
If points A, B, C and D are vertices of square then AB = BC = CD = AD where AB, BC, CD and AD are calculated using distance formula.
If points A, B and C are vertices of equilateral triangle then AB = BC = AC where AB, BC and AC are calculated using distance formula.
If points A, B and C are vertices of right-angle triangle, right angled at B then AB2 + BC2 = AC2 where AB, BC and AC are calculated using distance formula.
If points A, B and C are collinear then AB+BC=AC where AB, BC and AC are calculated using distance formula.
The distance of a point P(x, y) from the origin O(0, 0) is given by {tex}\mathrm{OP}=\sqrt{(x)^{2}+(y)^{2}}{/tex}.
Since distance is always non-negative, we take only the positive square root.
The distance between the points P(x1, y1 ) and Q(x2, y2 ) is {tex}P Q=\sqrt{\left(x_{2}-x_{1}\right)^{2}+\left(y_{2}-y_{1}\right)^{2}}{/tex}.
The distance of a point P(x, y) from the origin is {tex}\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}{/tex}.
The distance between two points P(x1, y1) and Q(x2, y2) is {tex}\sqrt{\left(x_{2}-x_{1}\right)^{2}+\left(y_{2}-y_{1}\right)^{2}}{/tex}.

Centroid of a triangle A (x1, y1) ,B (x2, y 2)   and C(x3, y3) is given by : {tex}\mathrm{O}=\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}_{1}+\mathrm{x}_{2}+\mathrm{x}_{3}}{3}, \frac{\mathrm{y}_{1}+\mathrm{y}_{2}+\mathrm{y}_{3}}{3}\right){/tex}
If two end points of diameter of circle are given then centre of circle is calculated using mid-point formula.
If three vertices of parallelogram are given then fourth vertex is calculated using mid-point formula.
The mid-point of the line segment joining the points A(x1, y1)  B(x2, y2) is {tex}\left(\frac{x_{2}+x_{1}}{2}, \frac{y_{2}+y_{1}}{2}\right){/tex}.
A(x1, y1)  B(x2, y2)  externally in the ratio m : n are {tex}P\left(x=\frac{m x_{2}-n x_{1}}{m-n}, y=\frac{m y_{2}-n y_{1}}{m-n}\right){/tex}

A(x1, y1)  B(x2, y 2) internally in the ratio m : n are {tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{mx_{2 } + n \mathrm { x } _ { 1 }}}{\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n}}, \mathrm{y}=\frac{\mathrm{m \textrm {y } _ { 2 } + \mathrm { n } \mathrm { } \mathrm { y } _ { 1 }}}{\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n}}\right){/tex}
The coordinates of the point P(x, y) which divides the line segment joining the points.
The mid-point of the line segment joining the points P(x1, y1) and Q(x2, y2) is {tex}\left(\frac{x_{1}+x_{2}}{2}, \frac{y_{1}+y_{2}}{2}\right){/tex}.

The coordinates of the point P(x, y) which divides the line segment joining the points A(x1, y1 ) and B(x2, y2) internally in the ratio m1 : m2 are {tex}\left(\frac{m_{1} x_{2}+m_{2} x_{1}}{m_{1}+m_{2}}, \frac{m_{1} y_{2}+m_{2} y_{1}}{m_{1}+m_{2}}\right){/tex}.
